title: Interstate 390 (New York)

infobox.value: [I-86](/wiki/Interstate_86_(New_York) "Interstate 86 (New York)") / [NY 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17") in [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(town),_New_York "Avoca (town), New York")

* [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20A in New York") in [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(town),_New_York "Geneseo (town), New York") * [US 20](/wiki/U.S._Route_20_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20 in New York") / [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") in [Avon](/wiki/Avon_(town),_New_York "Avon (town), New York") * [I-90 Toll](/wiki/Interstate_90_(New_York) "Interstate 90 (New York)") / [New York Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") / [NY 253](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_253 "New York State Route 253") in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") * [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590_(New_York) "Interstate 590 (New York)") near [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York")
[I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") / [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390") in [Gates](/wiki/Gates,_New_York "Gates, New York")

[Steuben](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York"), [Livingston](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), [Monroe](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York")

* [Main](/wiki/List_of_primary_Interstate_Highways "List of primary Interstate Highways") * [Auxiliary](/wiki/List_of_auxiliary_Interstate_Highways "List of auxiliary Interstate Highways") * [Suffixed](/wiki/List_of_suffixed_Interstate_Highways "List of suffixed Interstate Highways") * [Business](/wiki/List_of_business_routes_of_the_Interstate_Highway_System "List of business routes of the Interstate Highway System") * [Future](/wiki/List_of_future_Interstate_Highways "List of future Interstate Highways")  * **[New York Highways](/wiki/Numbered_highways_in_New_York "Numbered highways in New York")**  * [Interstate](/wiki/List_of_Interstate_Highways_in_New_York "List of Interstate Highways in New York") * [US](/wiki/List_of_U.S._Routes_in_New_York "List of U.S. Routes in New York") * [State](/wiki/List_of_state_routes_in_New_York "List of state routes in New York") * [Reference](/wiki/List_of_reference_routes_in_New_York "List of reference routes in New York") * [Parkways](/wiki/Parkways_in_New_York "Parkways in New York") | |

(**I-390**) is a 76.06-mile (122.41 km) north–south [auxiliary Interstate Highway](/wiki/Auxiliary_Interstate_Highway "Auxiliary Interstate Highway") located in the U.S. state of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). The southern terminus of the route is at the [Southern Tier Expressway](/wiki/Southern_Tier_Expressway "Southern Tier Expressway") ([I-86](/wiki/Interstate_86_in_New_York "Interstate 86 in New York") and [New York State Route 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17"), or NY 17) in the town of [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(town),_New_York "Avoca (town), New York"). Its northern terminus is at [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") (Western Expressway) in [Gates](/wiki/Gates,_New_York "Gates, New York"), just west of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). North of I-490, the expressway continues as [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390") to the [Lake Ontario State Parkway](/wiki/Lake_Ontario_State_Parkway "Lake Ontario State Parkway") in [Greece](/wiki/Greece_(town),_New_York "Greece (town), New York"). I-390 connects to its parent, [I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York") ([New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway")), in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"), a southern suburb of Rochester. The route is known as the **Genesee Expressway** from Avoca to [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590") in [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), where it becomes part of the [Rochester Outer Loop](/wiki/Rochester_Outer_Loop "Rochester Outer Loop").

(**NY 401**) in 1970 but redesignated as I-390 c. 1973. More sections of the freeway opened throughout the 1970s, and I-390 was extended northwestward to cover the southwestern quadrant of the Rochester Outer Loop in 1980 following the elimination of [NY 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47"). I-390 was completed in the early 1980s.

| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | ←  [NY 387](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_387 "New York State Route 387") |  | →  [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390") | | |

**Interstate 390** (**I-390**) is a 76.06-mile (122.41 km) north–south [auxiliary Interstate Highway](/wiki/Auxiliary_Interstate_Highway "Auxiliary Interstate Highway") located in the U.S. state of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). The southern terminus of the route is at the [Southern Tier Expressway](/wiki/Southern_Tier_Expressway "Southern Tier Expressway") ([I-86](/wiki/Interstate_86_in_New_York "Interstate 86 in New York") and [New York State Route 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17"), or NY 17) in the town of [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(town),_New_York "Avoca (town), New York"). Its northern terminus is at [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") (Western Expressway) in [Gates](/wiki/Gates,_New_York "Gates, New York"), just west of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). North of I-490, the expressway continues as [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390") to the [Lake Ontario State Parkway](/wiki/Lake_Ontario_State_Parkway "Lake Ontario State Parkway") in [Greece](/wiki/Greece_(town),_New_York "Greece (town), New York"). I-390 connects to its parent, [I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York") ([New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway")), in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"), a southern suburb of Rochester. The route is known as the **Genesee Expressway** from Avoca to [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590") in [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), where it becomes part of the [Rochester Outer Loop](/wiki/Rochester_Outer_Loop "Rochester Outer Loop").

In addition to serving Rochester, I-390 serves as an important connecting route between the [Rochester metropolitan area](/wiki/Rochester_metropolitan_area,_New_York "Rochester metropolitan area, New York") and [Corning](/wiki/Corning_(city),_New_York "Corning (city), New York") and the rest of the [Southern Tier](/wiki/Southern_Tier "Southern Tier") of New York. The freeway also serves several smaller communities between Corning and Rochester, including the [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") villages of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") and [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(village),_New_York "Geneseo (village), New York"). I-390 effectively replaced [US Route 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") (US 15) and parallels [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15"), US 15's de facto successor, north of Corning. From [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York") north to [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"), the two routes cross paths four times and are located no more than 1.5 miles (2.4 km) apart along the entire stretch.

The first section of the Genesee Expressway opened in the late 1960s, extending from [Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York") in the east to Dansville in the west. The entirety of the then-proposed highway was designated as **New York State Route 401** (**NY 401**) in 1970 but redesignated as I-390 c. 1973. More sections of the freeway opened throughout the 1970s, and I-390 was extended northwestward to cover the southwestern quadrant of the Rochester Outer Loop in 1980 following the elimination of [NY 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47"). I-390 was completed in the early 1980s.

## Route description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Interstate_390&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Route description")]

In its southern sections, which account for most of the highway's mileage, I-390 is a lightly trafficked freeway of two lanes in either direction going through minimally populated rural areas. Closer to Rochester, it becomes wider to accommodate the increased traffic it handles in the city itself.

### Steuben and Livingston counties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Interstate_390&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Steuben and Livingston counties")]

I-390 begins as westbound traffic on the concurrent routes of [I-86](/wiki/Interstate_86_(east) "Interstate 86 (east)") and [NY 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17") proceeds below the overpass carrying those two routes to the west, at an interchange just east of the village of [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(village),_New_York "Avoca (village), New York") in [Steuben County](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York"). The expressway heads northwest through the narrow valley of the [Cohocton River](/wiki/Cohocton_River "Cohocton River") to [Cohocton](/wiki/Cohocton_(village),_New_York "Cohocton (village), New York"), where the river leaves the expressway and heads north toward the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of Atlanta. I-390 climbs gradually as it continues past Cohocton, eventually turning due west as it descends to an interchange with [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21") south of [Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York").[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4) This exit is also the southern terminus of [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15"), which eventually parallels the Interstate further north.[[5]](#cite_note-2008tvr-5)

[![A divided expressway curving through a rural landscape alongside a valley, climbing slightly in the background.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/I-390_near_Cohocton%2C_NY.jpg/250px-I-390_near_Cohocton%2C_NY.jpg)](/wiki/File:I-390_near_Cohocton,_NY.jpg)

I-390 near Cohocton

The road climbs again as it makes a gentle bend and widens as it crosses the [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") line just south of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") and north of [Stony Brook State Park](/wiki/Stony_Brook_State_Park "Stony Brook State Park"). Descending again, it returns to its northwestern heading. North of the village, it climbs to the west side of the wide valley of [Canaseraga Creek](/wiki/Canaseraga_Creek "Canaseraga Creek"), giving the roadway a view over the area known locally as the Flats. [NY 36](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36 "New York State Route 36") begins to parallel the highway closely, with [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") on the far side of the Flats. Just south of the hamlet of [Sonyea](/wiki/Sonyea,_New_York "Sonyea, New York"), NY 36 crosses at a diamond interchange. I-390 turns northeast briefly but resumes its northwest course soon afterward. At this point, the highway follows more level ground, with woods and swamps mostly replacing farmland in the surrounding landscape.[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

[![A divided expressway curves from the right of the image through a mostly wooded landscape towards a ridgeline at the rear below a blue sky filled with little white fluffy clouds. Along the roadway closest to the camera is a large white truck with "Perry's Ice Cream" along the side and pictures of scoops of ice cream in various colors and flavors](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/I-390_near_Sonyea%2C_NY.jpg/250px-I-390_near_Sonyea%2C_NY.jpg)](/wiki/File:I-390_near_Sonyea,_NY.jpg)

I-390 northbound from exit 6 near Sonyea

After passing rest areas on either side, I-390 reaches the [NY 408](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_408 "New York State Route 408") exit adjacent to the [American Rock Salt](/w/index.php?title=American_Rock_Salt&action=edit&redlink=1 "American Rock Salt (page does not exist)") mine at [Hampton Corners](/w/index.php?title=Hampton_Corners,_New_York&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hampton Corners, New York (page does not exist)"). Here, most traffic bound for the [Buffalo](/wiki/Buffalo,_New_York "Buffalo, New York") area exits to go west to [Mount Morris](/wiki/Mount_Morris_(village),_New_York "Mount Morris (village), New York"), visible across the Flats from the freeway, where it follows NY 36 north. To the immediate east of the exit, NY 408 ends at NY 63, which carries traffic from the Interstate into [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(village),_New_York "Geneseo (village), New York"), the [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat"). A mile (1.6 km) north of the exit, Canaseraga Creek drains into the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River") on the road's west side.[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

The expressway continues north through Livingston County to [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(village),_New_York "Geneseo (village), New York"), which I-390 skirts to the south and east. While there is no exit for Geneseo on the part of the freeway that encroaches the village, exit 8, an interchange with [US 20 Alternate](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") (US 20A) east of Geneseo, provides the connection. Also accessible from the junction is the hamlet of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York"), situated off to the east at the junction of US 20A and NY 15. I-390 continues on, encountering NY 15 north of Lakeville and southeast of [Avon](/wiki/Avon_(village),_New_York "Avon (village), New York"). From here north to [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), I-390 and NY 15 follow closely parallel alignments. Both highways meet [US 20](/wiki/U.S._Route_20_in_New_York "U.S. Route 20 in New York") and [NY 5](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_5 "New York State Route 5") east of Avon prior to crossing into [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York").[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

### Monroe County

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Interstate_390&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Monroe County")]

As I-390 heads north through the southern extents of the county, the surroundings become more residential and commercial in nature. The freeway meets NY 15 and [NY 251](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_251 "New York State Route 251") in [Rush](/wiki/Rush,_New_York "Rush, New York") ahead of an interchange with both the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) and [NY 253](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_253 "New York State Route 253") in southern [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York"). Past the thruway, the transition from the rural landscapes of the Southern Tier and [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") to residential and commercial areas is completed as I-390 continues into the suburbs of Rochester and the commercial center of Henrietta. Roughly 1.5 miles (2.4 km) from NY 253, I-390 has an exit leading to Hylan Drive, a through street leading to [The Marketplace Mall](/wiki/The_Marketplace_Mall "The Marketplace Mall"). Farther north, I-390 meets both [NY 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A") and [NY 252](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_252 "New York State Route 252") in equally commercialized areas before entering [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York").[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Downtown_Rochester_NY_from_I-390_in_Henrietta.jpg/250px-Downtown_Rochester_NY_from_I-390_in_Henrietta.jpg)](/wiki/File:Downtown_Rochester_NY_from_I-390_in_Henrietta.jpg)

Northbound on I-390 in southern Henrietta. The skyline of [Downtown Rochester](/wiki/Downtown_Rochester "Downtown Rochester") is visible in the background.

Shortly after entering Brighton, I-390 crosses over the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") and meets the southern terminus of [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590") at a large [semi-directional T interchange](/wiki/Semi-directional_T_interchange "Semi-directional T interchange") located adjacent to the canal and South Clinton Avenue. The latter of the two passes through the eastern half of the junction, crossing over I-590 but passing under the ramps connecting I-590 south to I-390 south and I-390 north to I-590 north. Past the exit, I-390 turns to the west, joining the [Rochester Outer Loop](/wiki/Rochester_Outer_Loop "Rochester Outer Loop") and crossing back over the canal ahead of an interchange with NY 15 and NY 15A. After passing under NY 15, the freeway begins to run along the southern edge of the Erie Canal, here delimiting the southern city limits of Rochester. The canal and I-390 run side by side for just over one mile (1.6 km) to the Genesee River, where I-390 veers slightly to the west as it meets [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") at a [partial cloverleaf interchange](/wiki/Partial_cloverleaf_interchange "Partial cloverleaf interchange").[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dd/390sb_airport.jpg/250px-390sb_airport.jpg)](/wiki/File:390sb_airport.jpg)

I-390 southbound by the Greater Rochester International Airport, with heavy traffic on the northbound side

For the next mile (1.6 km), I-390 follows a more northwesterly alignment along the northeastern edge of the [Greater Rochester International Airport](/wiki/Greater_Rochester_International_Airport "Greater Rochester International Airport") grounds. It meets Brooks Avenue ([NY 204](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_204 "New York State Route 204")) at the northern edge of the grounds before leaving the airport area and curving more to the north, matching changes in the Erie Canal's alignment as both entities proceed around the southwestern edge of the city. Now in [Gates](/wiki/Gates,_New_York "Gates, New York"), I-390 crosses over the [Rochester and Southern Railroad](/wiki/Rochester_and_Southern_Railroad "Rochester and Southern Railroad") just south of another partial cloverleaf interchange with [NY 33A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_33A "New York State Route 33A"). The freeway continues on, passing under the [CSX Transportation](/wiki/CSX_Transportation "CSX Transportation")-owned [Rochester Subdivision](/wiki/Rochester_Subdivision "Rochester Subdivision") and [NY 33](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_33 "New York State Route 33") before connecting to [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") at a complex interchange roughly 0.25 miles (0.40 km) west of the canal and the Rochester city limits. I-390 ends here; however, the freeway continues northward toward [Greece](/wiki/Greece_(town),_New_York "Greece (town), New York") as [NY 390](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_390 "New York State Route 390").[[4]](#cite_note-bing-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Interstate_390&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [New York State Route 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47")

Construction on a [limited-access highway](/wiki/Limited-access_highway "Limited-access highway") between [Wayland](/wiki/Wayland_(village),_New_York "Wayland (village), New York") and [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") began c. 1966.[[6]](#cite_note-nbi-6) The highway opened to traffic c. 1968 as a realignment of [NY 245](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_245 "New York State Route 245").[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) On January 1, 1970, the expressway became part of NY 401, a designation that also extended south to the town of [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca,_New_York "Avoca, New York") and north to the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") along the Genesee Expressway,[[9]](#cite_note-1970log-9) a proposed freeway utilizing the [US 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") corridor.[[10]](#cite_note-10) NY 401 was added to the [Interstate Highway System](/wiki/Interstate_Highway_System "Interstate Highway System") and redesignated I-390 c. 1973.[[2]](#cite_note-1972map-2)[[3]](#cite_note-1973map-3) As initially planned, I-390 would continue north into Downtown Rochester and end at [I-490](/wiki/Interstate_490_(New_York) "Interstate 490 (New York)") while the entirety of the [Rochester Outer Loop](/wiki/Rochester_Outer_Loop "Rochester Outer Loop") south of I-490 would become [I-590](/wiki/Interstate_590 "Interstate 590").[[11]](#cite_note-1978nyt-11)[[12]](#cite_note-1977map-12)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Crw_3641.jpg/250px-Crw_3641.jpg)](/wiki/File:Crw_3641.jpg)

I-390 northbound near its interchange with I-590

Construction began c. 1974 on the portion of I-390 between [NY 17](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_17 "New York State Route 17") near the village of [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(village),_New_York "Avoca (village), New York") and [NY 21](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_21 "New York State Route 21") south of Wayland.[[3]](#cite_note-1973map-3)[[13]](#cite_note-13) It opened to traffic c. 1976.[[6]](#cite_note-nbi-6) By 1977, work had begun on the leg between [NY 251](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_251 "New York State Route 251") in [Rush](/wiki/Rush,_New_York "Rush, New York") and the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") ([I-90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) in [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York").[[12]](#cite_note-1977map-12) This section was completed by 1981, as were the Dansville–[Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") ([NY 36](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36 "New York State Route 36")), [Avon](/wiki/Avon_(town),_New_York "Avon (town), New York")–Rush, and thruway–Hylan Drive segments.[[6]](#cite_note-nbi-6)[[14]](#cite_note-1981map-14) The remainder of I-390 in [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York") up to the modern I-390/I-590 interchange was built c. 1981, and the final gap in the expressway from NY 36 to US 20 in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") opened c. 1982.[[6]](#cite_note-nbi-6)

By the late 1970s, in the face of community opposition, the proposed extension of I-390 into Downtown Rochester was scrapped.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Instead, the state of New York began to look into the possibility of changing the designations that were assigned to the outer loop. In one proposal submitted to the [American Association of State Highway and Transportation Officials](/wiki/American_Association_of_State_Highway_and_Transportation_Officials "American Association of State Highway and Transportation Officials") in the late 1970s, I-590 would be truncated to begin at the then-proposed junction with I-390 in [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"). The rest of the loop south of I-490, meanwhile, would become part of an extended I-390, which would continue north past I-490 to the outer loop's northwestern end at the [Lake Ontario State Parkway](/wiki/Lake_Ontario_State_Parkway "Lake Ontario State Parkway"). [NY 47](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_47 "New York State Route 47"),[[16]](#cite_note-1970s-req-16) the then-current designation for much of the outer loop,[[17]](#cite_note-17) would be eliminated.[[16]](#cite_note-1970s-req-16)

Most of the plans went into effect when the NY 47 designation was eliminated on March 18, 1980.[[18]](#cite_note-18) The southern half of the outer loop was signed as planned; however, I-390 and I-590 were modified to end at their junctions with I-490.[[19]](#cite_note-19) While NY 47 was officially assigned to the entirety of the outer loop prior to its removal,[[9]](#cite_note-1970log-9) only the portion from [NY 383](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_383 "New York State Route 383") north to I-490 was actually once part of NY 47.[[14]](#cite_note-1981map-14)

On February 10, 2008, whiteout conditions caused a 36-car pileup near the Scottsville Road exit, which led to a 17-year-old girl's death. Firefighters had to climb over cars and cut off roofs to get to people. More than 20 people were taken to the hospital.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

On July 17, 2011, a tour bus heading northbound from [Washington DC](/wiki/Washington_DC "Washington DC") to [Niagara Falls](/wiki/Niagara_Falls,_New_York "Niagara Falls, New York") crashed between the [Avoca](/wiki/Avoca_(village),_New_York "Avoca (village), New York") and [Cohocton](/wiki/Cohocton_(village),_New_York "Cohocton (village), New York") exits, killing two and injuring at least 35 others. Preliminary investigation listed a blown-out tire as a possible cause of the crash.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Future

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Interstate_390&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Future")]

As the successor to [US 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15 "U.S. Route 15") in [Western New York](/wiki/Western_New_York "Western New York"), it has been suggested that I-390 be added to the routes of either [I-99](/wiki/Interstate_99 "Interstate 99") or [I-83](/wiki/Interstate_83 "Interstate 83") as recently as 2002, with I-99's northern completed segment currently sharing the US 15 routing up to [Corning](/wiki/Corning,_New_York "Corning, New York"), 24 miles (39 km) south of I-390's current southern terminus. However, no official moves have been forwarded to fulfill this.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
